n02_astro_v5_scaffold.title: Scaffold Astro v5 project baseline and developer workflow
n02_astro_v5_scaffold.instruction: Goal: initialize a clean Astro project aligned with current docs and ready for implementation. In-scope: create project with `npm create astro@latest`, set TypeScript strictness, configure scripts (`dev`, `build`, `preview`, `astro check`), and commit baseline structure. Out-of-scope: feature pages, production styling, or content modeling. Verification: run `npm install && npm run astro check && npm run build` successfully. Artifacts: `package.json`, `astro.config.mjs`, `tsconfig.json`, `src/`, `.editorconfig`, `.gitignore`.
n02_astro_v5_scaffold.context: Reference: https://docs.astro.build/en/install-and-setup/ (Node prerequisite and create command). Version sanity check target: `astro@5.18.0`.
n02_astro_v5_scaffold.report: Revalidated and completed Astro baseline scaffold after the transient npm network blocker cleared. Confirmed scaffold files (package.json, astro.config.mjs, tsconfig.json, src/env.d.ts, src/pages/index.astro), ran npm install successfully, added @astrojs/check for non-interactive diagnostics, and verified npm run check (0 errors/warnings) plus npm run build (successful static build to dist/). Note: engine warnings remain because current Node is v20.17.0 while some deps request >=20.19.0, but checks and build pass.

n03_design_tokens_and_base_layout.title: Implement global design system and base layout from inspiration
n03_design_tokens_and_base_layout.instruction: Goal: establish reusable visual primitives so all pages inherit the same handcrafted romantic-yet-professional look. In-scope: CSS variables for colors/spacing/radius/shadows, typography pairing, textured background treatment, reusable paper-card wrappers, and motion primitives with reduced-motion fallbacks. Out-of-scope: final page-specific content and dynamic data rendering. Verification: run `npm run dev`, open a style playground route, and manually confirm tokens/motion/contrast on desktop + mobile. Artifacts: `src/styles/tokens.css`, `src/styles/global.css`, `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`, `src/components/ui/*`.
n03_design_tokens_and_base_layout.report:
Completed the global design system and base layout foundation for a romantic-yet-professional visual direction.

Implemented code changes:
1) Tokens and global styling
- Added `src/styles/tokens.css` with reusable variables for color palette, typography pairing, spacing scale, radii, shadows, motion durations/easing, and z-index.
- Added `src/styles/global.css` importing tokens and defining:
  - base/reset styles,
  - textured atmospheric background treatment,
  - typography hierarchy,
  - utility layout classes (`site-shell`, `stack`, `cluster`),
  - reusable surface primitives (`paper-card*`, `paper-deck`, `pill`),
  - motion primitives (`motion-reveal` variants and `motion-stagger`),
  - reduced-motion fallback via `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`.

2) Base layout
- Added `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`:
  - imports global styles,
  - centralizes shared HTML/head/meta/font loading,
  - provides reusable shell with skip-link, ambient background elements, and content slot.

3) Reusable UI components
- Added `src/components/ui/PaperCard.astro` (variant/padding/lift wrapper).
- Added `src/components/ui/PaperDeck.astro` (responsive card-grid wrapper).
- Added `src/components/ui/MotionReveal.astro` (single-element reveal with `kind` and `delay`).
- Added `src/components/ui/StaggerGroup.astro` (staggered entrance container with configurable step).
- Exported `Props` interfaces in Astro components to satisfy strict diagnostics.

4) Route wiring and playground
- Replaced `src/pages/index.astro` to consume the new layout + primitives and point to playground.
- Added `src/pages/style-playground.astro` for manual validation of tokens, card variants, and motion behavior.

Memory updates (required governance):
- `docs/memory/decision-log.md`: added DEC-001 for token-first visual foundation decision.
- `docs/memory/contracts.md`: added CON-001 internal interface contract for layout/style/component primitives.
- `docs/memory/risk-register.md`: added RISK-002 closure record confirming prior npm-registry blocker is now closed in practice.
- `docs/memory/evidence-index.md`: added EVD-002 with verification evidence and results.
- `docs/memory/changelog.md`: added CHG-002 with full changed-file inventory.

Lint/typecheck/build verification:
- Ran `npm run check` (initial run failed with ts(6196) unused `Props` in three Astro components; fixed by exporting interfaces).
- Reran `npm run check` -> PASS (0 errors/warnings/hints).
- Ran `npm run typecheck` -> PASS (0 errors/warnings/hints).
- Ran `npm run build` -> PASS; generated static routes `/` and `/style-playground/`.

Commit requirement handling:
- Files changed and commit was attempted as required by policy, but blocked because workspace is not a git repository.
- Evidence: `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` returned `fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git`.
- Per governance rule, node is not failed solely for this commit blocker.

Manual verification guidance:
- Run `npm run dev`, open `/style-playground`, and verify:
  - token consistency across cards/text,
  - hover elevation behavior,
  - reduced-motion behavior by toggling OS/browser setting,
  - visual contrast and spacing on desktop + mobile viewport sizes.

n04_content_collections_schema.title: Model portfolio and blog content with Astro Content Layer API
n04_content_collections_schema.instruction: Goal: define typed collections that support projects and blog posts with validation. In-scope: configure collections for `projects` and `blog` using `defineCollection`, `glob` loaders, and Zod schema (title, slug, dates, tags, summary, cover image, draft flags). Out-of-scope: final page rendering and SEO metadata wiring. Verification: run `npm run astro check` and confirm collection queries compile with no type errors. Artifacts: `src/content.config.ts`, `src/data/projects/*`, `src/data/blog/*`, shared content types/helpers.
n04_content_collections_schema.report:
Implemented Astro v5 Content Layer collections and typed schemas for `projects` and `blog`.

What changed
- Added `src/content.config.ts` defining two collections with `defineCollection` and `glob` loaders:
  - `projects` loader base: `./src/data/projects`, pattern: `**/*.{md,mdx}`
  - `blog` loader base: `./src/data/blog`, pattern: `**/*.{md,mdx}`
- Added shared schema module `src/content/schemas.ts` with reusable validation for:
  - `title`, `slug`, `summary`, `publishedAt`, `updatedAt`, `tags`, `coverImage`, `draft`
  - blog-specific `readingTimeMinutes`
  - project-specific `featured`, `repositoryUrl`, `demoUrl`
- Added shared query/type helpers in `src/lib/content.ts`:
  - `ProjectEntry`, `BlogEntry` type aliases from `CollectionEntry`
  - `getPublishedProjects()` and `getPublishedBlogPosts()` filtered on `!draft` and sorted by most recent `updatedAt || publishedAt`
- Added seed content entries:
  - `src/data/projects/atelier-scheduler.md`
  - `src/data/projects/letterpress-notes.md`
  - `src/data/blog/astro-content-layer-in-practice.md`
  - `src/data/blog/tuning-page-motion-with-respect.md`

Memory governance updates
- Updated `docs/memory/contracts.md` with `[CON-002] Astro Content Layer Collections Contract`.
- Updated `docs/memory/evidence-index.md` with `[EVD-003] Content Collections Schema Verification`.
- Updated `docs/memory/changelog.md` with `[CHG-003] Content Layer Collections and Seed Data`.
- All added memory entries include NodeID, Date, Author, Change, and Status.

Lint/typecheck and verification
- Lint: no dedicated lint script is configured in `package.json` for this repo scope.
- Type/content checks run:
  1) `npm run astro check` initially failed once with Astro datastore ENOENT while checks were started in parallel (`rename ... node_modules/.astro/data-store.json.tmp -> .../data-store.json`).
  2) `npm run typecheck` initially failed on `src/lib/content.ts` generic typing around `updatedAt` optionality.
  3) Fixed helper type in `src/lib/content.ts` (`updatedAt?: Date | undefined`).
  4) Re-ran sequentially:
     - `npm run astro check` -> pass (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints)
     - `npm run typecheck` -> pass (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints)

Commit requirement status
- Attempted to create the required single commit, but workspace is not a git repository.
- Command run: `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`
- Result: `fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git`
- Per governance, node is not failed solely for this commit blocker.

Manual verification
- Run `npm run astro check` and `npm run typecheck` from repo root.
- Query functions can be imported from `src/lib/content.ts` to consume typed published entries from both collections.

n05_initial_content_seed.title: Seed first-pass portfolio and blog content
n05_initial_content_seed.instruction: Goal: create enough real content to drive final IA, visual fidelity, and launch readiness. In-scope: extract professional profile inputs from LinkedIn/CV, map inspiration motifs from `inspo/*.html` into portfolio-safe copy/image direction, add Manuel profile/about data, at least 3 project entries, and at least 2 blog draft posts with frontmatter matching schemas; normalize external links and media placeholders. Out-of-scope: full editorial refinement or external CMS integration. Verification: run `npm run astro check` and manually verify all entries load via collection queries without fallback content. Artifacts: `src/data/projects/*.md`, `src/data/blog/*.md|*.mdx`, `src/data/profile.json` (or equivalent), `docs/content-sourcing-notes.md`.
n05_initial_content_seed.report:
Completed `n05_initial_content_seed` by seeding first-pass portfolio/profile/blog content and documenting sourcing direction.

What I changed
- Seeded profile/about payload:
  - `src/data/profile.json`
  - Added canonical first-pass fields: `name`, `headline`, `location`, `timezone`, `aboutShort`, `aboutLong`, `focusAreas`, `contact`, `social`, `availability`.
- Expanded project content (>= 3 entries total achieved: now 4):
  - Updated `src/data/projects/atelier-scheduler.md` (removed sample-domain demo URL and refined body copy).
  - Updated `src/data/projects/letterpress-notes.md` (`updatedAt` + promoted to `draft: false` + richer body copy).
  - Added `src/data/projects/field-journal-cms.md`.
  - Added `src/data/projects/signalboard-analytics.md`.
- Expanded blog draft content (>= 2 draft posts achieved):
  - Added `src/data/blog/from-scrapbook-motifs-to-portfolio-language.md` (`draft: true`).
  - Existing `src/data/blog/tuning-page-motion-with-respect.md` remains `draft: true`.
- Added sourcing and motif translation doc:
  - `docs/content-sourcing-notes.md`
  - Included mapping from `inspo/*.html` motifs to portfolio-safe copy/image direction, plus link/media normalization rules.

Rationale
- Node goal was to provide enough real content to drive IA/visual fidelity and launch readiness without full editorial polish.
- Content was aligned to existing collection schemas (`projects` + `blog`) and normalized to avoid sample-domain placeholders.
- Added profile data so downstream page implementation can consume a stable, explicit about/profile source.

Memory governance updates
- Updated `docs/memory/contracts.md` with:
  - `[CON-003] Profile Seed and Content Baseline Contract`
- Updated `docs/memory/evidence-index.md` with:
  - `[EVD-004] Initial Portfolio and Blog Content Seed Verification`
- Updated `docs/memory/changelog.md` with:
  - `[CHG-004] Initial Portfolio Content and Profile Seed`
- All new entries include NodeID, Date, Author, Change, and Status.

Lint/typecheck and verification
- Lint:
  - No dedicated lint script is configured in this repo scope (`npm run` lists `dev`, `build`, `preview`, `astro`, `check`, `typecheck` only).
- Type/content checks run:
  1) `npm run check` -> pass (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints)
  2) `npm run typecheck` -> pass (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints)
  3) Re-ran `npm run check` after edits finalization -> pass (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints)
- Additional verification:
  - `npm run build` -> pass
  - Content quality scan: `(rg -n "example\.com|TODO|TBD|lorem ipsum|placeholder" src/data docs/content-sourcing-notes.md && echo matches_found) || echo no_placeholder_matches` -> `no_placeholder_matches`
  - Content volume checks:
    - `projects=4`
    - `blog=3`
    - `blog_drafts=2`

Manual verification notes
- Collection schemas validate all newly added/edited entries during Astro checks.
- Seeded files are ready for collection-driven rendering and avoid fallback/sample content markers.

Commit requirement status
- Attempted to validate git workspace for required node commit.
- Command: `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`
- Result: `fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git`
- Per governance, task is not failed solely for this commit blocker.

Known follow-up
- `docs/content-sourcing-notes.md` flags final editorial verification of profile copy against the latest external LinkedIn/CV source of truth before public launch.

n06_site_shell_navigation.title: Build responsive site shell and navigation system
n06_site_shell_navigation.instruction: Goal: provide cohesive routing and consistent chrome for all sections. In-scope: header/nav/footer, active-route states, mobile menu, skip-link, and shared page layout slots. Out-of-scope: detailed content sections for home/projects/blog pages. Verification: run `npm run dev` and manually verify navigation, keyboard focus order, and mobile behavior across core routes. Artifacts: `src/components/layout/Header.astro`, `src/components/layout/Footer.astro`, `src/components/layout/Nav.astro`, layout wiring updates.
n06_site_shell_navigation.report:
Implemented node `n06_site_shell_navigation` as a single coherent shell/navigation integration across shared layout, styling, and route scaffolds.

What changed
1) New shared layout components
- Added `src/components/layout/Nav.astro`:
  - Centralized primary nav items (`/`, `/about`, `/projects`, `/blog`, `/contact`).
  - Computes active route state from `currentPath` with prefix-aware matching for section paths.
  - Applies `aria-current="page"` to the active link.
  - Provides responsive mobile collapse behavior using semantic `<details>/<summary>`.
- Added `src/components/layout/Header.astro`:
  - Brand link + tagline + `Nav` wiring.
- Added `src/components/layout/Footer.astro`:
  - Shared footer copy and key links.

2) Base layout shell wiring
- Updated `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`:
  - Imports and renders shared `Header` and `Footer` around all page content.
  - Uses `Astro.url.pathname` to pass current path into nav.
  - Preserves skip-link and maps it to `#main-content`.
  - Introduces page layout slots: named `hero` slot and default content slot container.

3) Global styling for responsive shell/navigation
- Updated `src/styles/global.css` with:
  - Shell/frame structure (`site-frame`, `site-main`, `site-main__inner`, `site-main__content`).
  - Sticky translucent header, navigation link states, mobile nav panel, and footer styling.
  - Focus-visible treatment for links/summary controls.
  - Mobile breakpoints for nav/menu behavior and shell spacing.

4) Route wiring updates and core scaffolds
- Updated existing routes to use hero slot:
  - `src/pages/index.astro`
  - `src/pages/style-playground.astro`
- Added minimal scaffold routes (navigation/layout verification only, no detailed content):
  - `src/pages/about.astro`
  - `src/pages/contact.astro`
  - `src/pages/projects/index.astro`
  - `src/pages/blog/index.astro`

Memory governance updates
- Updated `docs/memory/decision-log.md` with DEC-002.
- Updated `docs/memory/contracts.md` with CON-004.
- Updated `docs/memory/evidence-index.md` with EVD-005.
- Updated `docs/memory/changelog.md` with CHG-005.
- All new memory entries include NodeID, Date, Author, Change, Status.

Lint/typecheck verification (required)
- Ran: `npm run check` -> PASS (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
- Ran: `npm run typecheck` -> PASS (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
- Reran both commands after final edits/memory updates to confirm no regressions; both remained PASS.

Commit requirement handling
- Attempted required single commit (`node(n06_site_shell_navigation): build responsive shell and navigation`) but repository commit operations are blocked because workspace is not a git repository.
- Evidence commands/results:
  - `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` -> `fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git`
  - `git add ... && git commit ...` -> same fatal error.
- Per governance rule, node is not failed solely due this environment-level commit blocker.

Manual verification steps
1. Run `npm run dev`.
2. Visit `/`, `/about`, `/projects`, `/blog`, `/contact`, and `/style-playground`.
3. Verify:
   - header/footer persist across routes,
   - active nav state updates correctly,
   - keyboard focus order includes skip-link then primary nav,
   - mobile menu collapses/expands via summary toggle,
   - skip-link lands at `#main-content`.

n07_home_about_contact_pages.title: Implement Home, About, and Contact pages using real content
n07_home_about_contact_pages.instruction: Goal: ship the core personal-brand pages that communicate identity and conversion points. In-scope: homepage hero/story/highlights, about narrative, and contact section with mail/social links and clear CTA hierarchy; preserve inspiration aesthetics in a professional portfolio framing. Out-of-scope: projects listing details and blog index/post routing. Verification: run `npm run dev` and manually validate copy hierarchy, responsive layout, and CTA accessibility at mobile/tablet/desktop sizes. Artifacts: `src/pages/index.astro`, `src/pages/about.astro`, `src/pages/contact.astro`, page-specific components.
n07_home_about_contact_pages.report:
Completed `n07_home_about_contact_pages` by replacing route scaffolds with real-content implementations for home/about/contact and introducing dedicated page components aligned to the existing editorial shell.

What I changed
1) Homepage implementation (`src/pages/index.astro`)
- Replaced the scaffold content with a data-backed page composition.
- Integrated canonical profile content from `src/data/profile.json`.
- Integrated collection signals via `getPublishedProjects()` and `getPublishedBlogPosts()`.
- Added fallback-safe highlight generation and wired new page sections:
  - `HomeHero` (hero + primary/secondary CTAs + featured highlights)
  - `HomeStory` (narrative + focus areas + availability)
  - `HomeHighlights` (projects/blog metrics and directional CTAs)

2) About page implementation (`src/pages/about.astro`)
- Replaced placeholder copy with real narrative and collaboration framing.
- Wired page-specific components:
  - `AboutHero` (positioning + location/timezone context)
  - `AboutNarrative` (long-form narrative, focus areas, operating-principles timeline)
- Added a dedicated CTA section driving users to `/contact`.

3) Contact page implementation (`src/pages/contact.astro`)
- Replaced placeholder route with conversion-focused contact structure.
- Wired page-specific components:
  - `ContactHero` (intro framing)
  - `ContactChannels` (clear CTA hierarchy)
- Implemented explicit action priority:
  - Primary CTA: email inquiry (`mailto:`)
  - Secondary CTAs: LinkedIn and GitHub
- Added guidance card for what information to include in outreach.

4) New page-specific components added
- `src/components/pages/home/HomeHero.astro`
- `src/components/pages/home/HomeStory.astro`
- `src/components/pages/home/HomeHighlights.astro`
- `src/components/pages/about/AboutHero.astro`
- `src/components/pages/about/AboutNarrative.astro`
- `src/components/pages/contact/ContactHero.astro`
- `src/components/pages/contact/ContactChannels.astro`

Rationale
- The node required production-ready core pages with real content and strong CTA hierarchy while preserving the established handcrafted/editorial aesthetic.
- Splitting sections into page-specific components keeps route files clean, supports future iteration, and avoids reintroducing scaffold duplication.
- Contact page hierarchy was designed to make conversion intent explicit and accessible.

Memory governance updates
- Updated `docs/memory/contracts.md` with:
  - `[CON-005] Core Page Content and CTA Hierarchy Contract`
- Updated `docs/memory/evidence-index.md` with:
  - `[EVD-006] Home/About/Contact Content Page Verification`
- Updated `docs/memory/changelog.md` with:
  - `[CHG-006] Real Content for Home, About, and Contact Pages`
- Each entry includes NodeID, Date, Author, Change, and Status.

Lint/typecheck verification (required)
- Lint command availability:
  - No dedicated `lint` script exists in this repo scope (`package.json` scripts are `dev`, `build`, `preview`, `astro`, `check`, `typecheck`).
- Commands run:
  1) `npm run check` -> initially failed with 2 TypeScript diagnostics in `src/pages/index.astro` (undefined guard and exact optional props).
  2) Applied fix in `src/pages/index.astro` (guarded fallback project access and provided explicit fallback strings).
  3) `npm run check` -> pass (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
  4) `npm run typecheck` -> pass (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
  5) Additional confidence check: `npm run build` -> pass; static routes generated including `/`, `/about`, `/contact`.

Manual verification steps
1. Run `npm run dev`.
2. Open `/`, `/about`, `/contact`.
3. Validate:
- Home has hero/story/highlights with real data and clear CTA sequence.
- About has narrative + focus areas + operating-principles timeline.
- Contact has primary email CTA and secondary social links.
- Layout remains responsive across mobile/tablet/desktop widths.
- Keyboard focus flow and link targets are accessible.

Commit requirement status
- Attempted required single node commit: `node(n07_home_about_contact_pages): implement home about contact content pages`.
- Blocked by workspace git state:
  - `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` -> `fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git`
  - `git add ... && git commit ...` -> same fatal error.
- Per governance rule, node is not failed solely due this environment-level commit blocker.

Out-of-scope preserved
- Projects listing details and blog index/post routing were not expanded beyond node scope; homepage highlights link to `/projects` and `/blog` section indexes only.

n08_projects_index_and_detail.title: Build projects listing and dynamic project detail pages
n08_projects_index_and_detail.instruction: Goal: present portfolio work with clear outcomes, stack, and narrative depth. In-scope: projects archive page, dynamic detail route generation from collections, reusable project card/details blocks, and graceful handling for missing optional fields. Out-of-scope: blog routes and RSS generation. Verification: run `npm run build` and manually open generated project routes in preview to confirm all entries render and links resolve. Artifacts: `src/pages/projects/index.astro`, `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro`, `src/components/projects/*`.
n08_projects_index_and_detail.report:
Completed `n08_projects_index_and_detail` by replacing the projects scaffold with a collection-driven archive and adding dynamic static detail routes, plus reusable project presentation components.

What I changed
1) Implemented reusable projects UI blocks
- Added `src/components/projects/ProjectCard.astro`:
  - Reusable card used on archive and related-project sections.
  - Handles optional fields gracefully:
    - `coverImage` missing -> gradient placeholder media block.
    - `updatedAt` missing -> omitted from card meta row.
    - `repositoryUrl`/`demoUrl` missing -> only render available links.
  - Includes project title/summary/tags/date metadata and links to `/projects/<slug>/`.
- Added `src/components/projects/ProjectDetailsPanel.astro`:
  - Reusable details block for project metadata on detail pages.
  - Handles optional fields gracefully:
    - `updatedAt` missing -> displays `Not provided`.
    - no external links -> explicit fallback note.

2) Built projects archive page from content collection
- Updated `src/pages/projects/index.astro`:
  - Replaced scaffold copy with production archive.
  - Uses `getPublishedProjects()` for data source.
  - Adds summary hero with published/featured counts.
  - Renders all projects using `ProjectCard` in a staggered responsive grid.
  - Includes explicit empty-state fallback if no published projects are present.

3) Added dynamic project detail route generation
- Added `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro`:
  - Implements `getStaticPaths()` from `getPublishedProjects()` using `project.data.slug`.
  - Uses `render(project)` from `astro:content` to render markdown narrative body.
  - Adds detail hero + tags + back link to `/projects`.
  - Uses `ProjectDetailsPanel` for metadata/links.
  - Adds related-project section reusing `ProjectCard`.

Rationale
- This keeps the projects archive and detail routes fully synchronized with typed collection content.
- Reusable `src/components/projects/*` blocks reduce duplication and establish a stable UI contract for future content expansion.
- Optional-field fallbacks avoid runtime/content rendering gaps when metadata is partially omitted.

Memory governance updates
- Updated `docs/memory/decision-log.md`:
  - Added `[DEC-003] Collection-Driven Projects Routes with Shared Card/Detail Blocks`.
- Updated `docs/memory/contracts.md`:
  - Added `[CON-006] Projects Archive and Dynamic Detail Route Contract`.
- Updated `docs/memory/evidence-index.md`:
  - Added `[EVD-007] Projects Archive and Dynamic Detail Routes Verification`.
- Updated `docs/memory/changelog.md`:
  - Added `[CHG-007] Projects Index and Dynamic Detail Route Implementation`.
- All entries include NodeID, Date, Author, Change, Status.

Lint/typecheck/build verification (required)
- Ran `npm run check` (initial run):
  - Found 2 Astro/TS errors in `[slug].astro` (`project.render()` mismatch and unused `Props`).
- Applied in-scope fix:
  - Switched to `render(project)` via `astro:content`.
  - Typed props usage via `Astro.props as Props`.
- Re-ran `npm run check`: PASS (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
- Ran `npm run typecheck`: PASS (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
- Re-ran both after memory updates:
  - `npm run check`: PASS.
  - `npm run typecheck`: PASS.
- Ran acceptance build command:
  - `npm run build`: PASS.
  - Build generated `/projects/index.html` and all dynamic detail pages:
    - `/projects/atelier-scheduler/`
    - `/projects/field-journal-cms/`
    - `/projects/letterpress-notes/`
    - `/projects/signalboard-analytics/`

Manual/preview verification notes
- Attempted `npm run preview -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4322` for route open checks, but sandbox blocked port bind with `listen EPERM`.
- Fallback verification performed via generated artifacts:
  - confirmed route files exist under `dist/projects/*/index.html`.
  - confirmed archive links resolve to all slug routes and detail pages include expected navigation/details sections and external links where present.

Commit requirement status
- Attempted git workspace validation for mandatory node commit.
- Command: `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`
- Result: `fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git`
- Per governance, node not failed solely for this environment-level commit blocker.

Known limitations/follow-up
- Manual browser-open verification in `astro preview` must be performed in an environment that permits local port binding; implementation and static artifact validation are complete.

n09_blog_index_post_and_tags.title: Build blog index, post pages, and tag navigation
n09_blog_index_post_and_tags.instruction: Goal: deliver a full blog section where Manuel can publish and browse writing. In-scope: blog index with sorting/pagination strategy, individual post route rendering from collection content, tag taxonomy pages, and draft filtering behavior for production builds. Out-of-scope: RSS endpoint and sitemap integration. Verification: run `npm run build && npm run preview` and manually verify post routes, tag routes, and draft exclusion in production output. Artifacts: `src/pages/blog/index.astro`, `src/pages/blog/[slug].astro`, `src/pages/blog/tags/[tag].astro`, blog components/utilities.
n09_blog_index_post_and_tags.report:
Completed `n09_blog_index_post_and_tags` by replacing the blog scaffold with a full collection-driven blog section (index, post detail, tag taxonomy, and pagination routes) plus production-safe draft filtering behavior.

What I changed
1) Blog content utilities (`src/lib/content.ts`)
- Added `getBlogPosts({ includeDrafts? })` that defaults to environment-aware behavior (`!import.meta.env.PROD`), so drafts are included during non-production preview workflows and excluded in production builds.
- Added `getBlogTagSummaries()` to aggregate/sort tags and generate canonical tag-route hrefs.
- Added `BLOG_PAGE_SIZE` and generic `paginateEntries()` to enforce deterministic pagination metadata/hrefs for blog archives.
- Kept existing project/blog published helper compatibility.

2) New reusable blog components
- Added `src/components/blog/BlogCard.astro`:
  - Standardized post preview card (cover fallback, publish/update/read-time meta, draft badge, tag chips linking to tag routes).
- Added `src/components/blog/BlogPagination.astro`:
  - Reusable prev/next controls with page status output.

3) Blog routes implementation
- Updated `src/pages/blog/index.astro`:
  - Replaced placeholder scaffold with real index listing.
  - Added newest-first archive rendering, tag taxonomy section, and page-1 pagination controls.
  - Included explicit pagination strategy signal (`BLOG_PAGE_SIZE`) and environment draft visibility indicator.
- Added `src/pages/blog/[slug].astro`:
  - Dynamic static path generation from blog collection entries.
  - Markdown rendering for full post body with hero metadata and tag links.
  - Related-post section based on overlapping tags.
- Added `src/pages/blog/tags/[tag].astro`:
  - Dynamic tag archive route generation from discovered tags.
  - Per-tag listing with sibling tag navigation.
- Added `src/pages/blog/page/[page].astro`:
  - Static paginated archive route generation for pages > 1.
  - Uses shared card and pagination components for consistency.

Rationale
- The node required a complete writing section with collection-backed routing and taxonomy.
- Centralizing routing helpers in `src/lib/content.ts` keeps index/detail/tag/pagination routes in sync and avoids divergent draft/filter logic.
- Environment-aware draft behavior satisfies the requirement to exclude drafts in production output while preserving editorial visibility during non-production work.

Memory governance updates
- `docs/memory/decision-log.md`: appended `[DEC-004] Environment-Aware Blog Route Generation with Shared Entry Components`.
- `docs/memory/contracts.md`: appended `[CON-007] Blog Index, Post, Tag, and Pagination Route Contract`.
- `docs/memory/evidence-index.md`: appended `[EVD-008] Blog Index/Post/Tag Route Verification`.
- `docs/memory/changelog.md`: appended `[CHG-008] Blog Index, Post, Tag, and Pagination Route Implementation`.
- All entries include NodeID, Date, Author, Change, and Status.

Lint/typecheck/build verification (required)
- Lint command availability:
  - No dedicated lint script is configured in this repo scope (`npm run` exposes `check` and `typecheck`, both mapping to `astro check`).
- Commands run:
  1) `npm run check` -> initially failed with exact optional property typing issues in pagination props; fixed type contracts in `src/lib/content.ts`, `src/components/blog/BlogPagination.astro`, and `src/pages/blog/page/[page].astro`.
  2) `npm run check` (rerun) -> PASS (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
  3) `npm run typecheck` -> PASS (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
  4) `npm run build` -> PASS; generated blog routes include published post/tag pages.
  5) Post-memory-update reruns: `npm run check` PASS and `npm run typecheck` PASS.

Verification of acceptance behavior
- Attempted runtime preview per node verification:
  - `npm run preview -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4322` -> failed in sandbox with `listen EPERM` (port bind not permitted).
- Static artifact fallback verification (production output):
  - `test -f dist/blog/astro-content-layer-in-practice/index.html` -> pass.
  - `test -f dist/blog/tags/astro/index.html` -> pass.
  - `test ! -e dist/blog/tuning-page-motion-with-respect/index.html` -> pass (draft post excluded).
  - `test ! -e dist/blog/tags/accessibility/index.html` -> pass (draft-only tag excluded).

  - `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` -> `fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git`
- Per governance rule, node is not failed solely due this environment-level commit blocker.

Manual verification steps

2) If environment permits local binding, run `npm run preview` and open:
   - `/blog`
   - `/blog/astro-content-layer-in-practice/`
   - `/blog/tags/astro/`
3) Confirm draft entries are absent in production build output (no route for `/blog/tuning-page-motion-with-respect/`).

n10_mdx_authoring_workflow.title: Enable MDX authoring and reusable blog content components
n10_mdx_authoring_workflow.instruction: Goal: make long-form writing flexible so blog posts can include richer components when needed. In-scope: install/configure `@astrojs/mdx`, ensure collections loader includes `.mdx`, create a small set of reusable prose components (callout, image figure, code note), and add one MDX sample post. Out-of-scope: introducing external CMS or visual editor tooling. Verification: run `npm run astro check && npm run build` and confirm sample MDX post renders with custom components. Artifacts: `astro.config.mjs`, `src/content.config.ts`, `src/components/prose/*`, one `.mdx` post.
n10_mdx_authoring_workflow.report:
Completed `n10_mdx_authoring_workflow` by enabling Astro MDX, adding reusable prose components, wiring MDX component rendering for blog posts, and adding a published sample `.mdx` entry.

What changed
1) MDX integration setup
- Updated `astro.config.mjs` to include `@astrojs/mdx` integration via `integrations: [mdx()]`.

2) Reusable prose component set
- Added `src/components/prose/Callout.astro` with tone variants (`warm`, `cool`, `caution`) and title/body slot structure.
- Added `src/components/prose/ImageFigure.astro` for consistent figure/caption rendering.
- Added `src/components/prose/CodeNote.astro` for structured code-callout blocks with language/title props.

3) Blog rendering integration
- Updated `src/pages/blog/[slug].astro` to import `Callout`, `ImageFigure`, and `CodeNote`.
- Added shared mapping object `const proseComponents = { Callout, ImageFigure, CodeNote };`.
- Render call changed to `<Content components={proseComponents} />` so MDX posts can use custom components directly.

4) Sample MDX post
- Added `src/data/blog/mdx-authoring-with-shared-prose-components.mdx` (published, non-draft) demonstrating all three components in one long-form post.

Rationale
- Keeps blog authoring flexible for long-form writing while preserving visual consistency through shared, reusable prose blocks.
- Component mapping at route-render level avoids one-off component setup per post and keeps `.mdx` author experience simple.

n11_seo_rss_sitemap_metadata.title: Implement SEO baseline, RSS feed, and sitemap
n11_seo_rss_sitemap_metadata.instruction: Goal: ensure discoverability and subscription support for portfolio and blog content. In-scope: set `site` URL in Astro config, canonical/meta/OpenGraph defaults, add RSS endpoint via `@astrojs/rss`, wire feed autodiscovery link, and add `@astrojs/sitemap` integration. Out-of-scope: advanced analytics dashboards or search indexing tooling. Verification: run `npm run build` and confirm output includes `rss.xml`, `sitemap-index.xml`, and valid metadata on key pages. Artifacts: `astro.config.mjs`, `src/pages/rss.xml.js|ts`, shared SEO component(s), layout head updates.
n11_seo_rss_sitemap_metadata.report:
Completed `n11_seo_rss_sitemap_metadata` by implementing SEO baseline metadata defaults, RSS feed generation, and sitemap integration.

Implementation summary
1) Astro site + sitemap integration
- Updated `astro.config.mjs`:
  - Added `site: "https://manuelcecchetto.it"`.
  - Added `@astrojs/sitemap` integration (`sitemap()`) alongside existing `mdx()`.

2) Shared SEO metadata defaults and discovery links
- Updated `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`:
  - Added typed SEO props: `ogType`, `image`, `noindex`.
  - Added canonical URL generation from `Astro.url.pathname` + `Astro.site` fallback.
  - Added baseline head metadata: `description`, `robots`, `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:type`, `og:url`, `og:site_name`, `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`.
  - Added optional image enrichment for OG/Twitter when `image` is provided.
  - Added feed autodiscovery link: `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" ... href=".../rss.xml">`.
  - Added sitemap discovery link: `<link rel="sitemap" href=".../sitemap-index.xml">`.

3) RSS endpoint
- Added `src/pages/rss.xml.ts`:
  - Implemented `GET` endpoint using `@astrojs/rss`.
  - Feed source: `getBlogPosts({ includeDrafts: false })`.
  - Items map to `/blog/<slug>/` with title/summary/publish date.
  - Added `<language>en-us</language>` feed metadata.

4) Page-level OG type/image wiring on detail routes
- Updated `src/pages/blog/[slug].astro`: pass `ogType="article"` and `image={post.data.coverImage}`.
- Updated `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro`: pass `ogType="article"` and `image={project.data.coverImage}`.

Rationale
- Centralizing metadata defaults in `BaseLayout` prevents route-by-route SEO drift.
- Setting `site` in config ensures canonical and sitemap generation resolve to the production origin.
- Generating RSS from published blog entries provides subscription support without exposing drafts.

Memory governance updates
- `docs/memory/decision-log.md`: appended `[DEC-006] Site-Level SEO Baseline via Shared Head Metadata and Discovery Endpoints`.
- `docs/memory/contracts.md`: appended `[CON-009] SEO Metadata, RSS Feed, and Sitemap Output Contract`.
- `docs/memory/risk-register.md`: appended `[RISK-006] RSS/Sitemap Dependency Blocker Closed for Node Scope`.
- `docs/memory/evidence-index.md`: appended `[EVD-012] SEO Baseline, RSS, and Sitemap Verification`.
- `docs/memory/changelog.md`: appended `[CHG-012] SEO Metadata Baseline, RSS Feed, and Sitemap Integration`.
- Each entry includes NodeID, Date, Author, Change, and Status.

Required lint/typecheck/build verification

- `npm run typecheck` -> PASS (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
- `npm run build` -> PASS.
  - Build output includes route generation for `src/pages/rss.xml.ts` (`/rss.xml`).
  - `@astrojs/sitemap` generated `dist/sitemap-index.xml`.
- Additional assertions:
  - `ls -1 dist | rg 'rss.xml|sitemap'` -> `rss.xml`, `sitemap-0.xml`, `sitemap-index.xml`.
  - `rg -n "canonical|og:title|application/rss\+xml|rel=\"sitemap\"" dist/index.html` confirms metadata + discovery tags are present in rendered head.

n12_performance_and_accessibility_pass.title: Run performance and accessibility hardening pass
n12_performance_and_accessibility_pass.instruction: Goal: elevate production quality before release. In-scope: optimize image loading strategy, audit font loading, enforce reduced-motion behavior, improve contrast/focus states, and reduce layout shift in core templates. Out-of-scope: redesigning IA or changing core visual identity. Verification: run `npm run build && npm run preview`, perform manual keyboard/screen-reader smoke test, and record Lighthouse checks for Home, Project detail, and Blog post pages. Artifacts: updated page/components/styles plus `docs/qa/perf-a11y-report.md`.
n12_performance_and_accessibility_pass.report:
Completed `n12_performance_and_accessibility_pass` with a focused performance/a11y hardening pass across core templates and shared styling.

What I changed
1) Image loading strategy + CLS reduction
- Updated reusable media components with explicit dimensions/sizes and loading priority hints:
  - `src/components/blog/BlogCard.astro`
  - `src/components/projects/ProjectCard.astro`
  - `src/components/prose/ImageFigure.astro`
- Added `decoding="async"` and `fetchpriority` policy:
  - card/prose media: `loading="lazy"`, `fetchpriority="low"`
- Stabilized card media layout by replacing `min-height` with fixed `aspect-ratio: 16 / 10` in blog/project cards.
- Updated detail template hero covers in:
  - `src/pages/blog/[slug].astro`
  - `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro`
  - Added `loading="eager"`, `fetchpriority="high"`, explicit width/height, and `sizes` hints.
  - Added per-page `<link rel="preload" as="image" ...>` via `slot="head"` for detail hero covers.

2) Font loading audit and optimization
- Updated `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`:
  - centralized Google Fonts URL in a constant
  - added `dns-prefetch` hints for font origins
  - retained `preconnect`
  - added stylesheet `preload` for earlier discovery

3) Reduced-motion hardening
- Updated `src/styles/global.css` reduced-motion block to explicitly disable hover transforms for:
  - lifted cards
  - CTA hover lift
  - blog/project media hover zoom
- Disabled ambient decorative blobs under reduced-motion mode.

4) Contrast/focus improvements
- Updated tokens in `src/styles/tokens.css`:
  - darkened `--color-text-muted` for better contrast
  - added `--color-focus-ring` and `--color-focus-glow`
- Updated global focus styling in `src/styles/global.css`:
  - thicker, higher-contrast `:focus-visible` outline + glow halo
  - increased underline thickness for links
  - improved footer link contrast
  - added font metric stabilization via `font-size-adjust` for body/headings to reduce perceived swap shift
- Updated `src/components/pages/home/HomeHero.astro`:
  - CTA hover movement restricted to fine pointers (`@media (hover: hover) and (pointer: fine)`)
  - CTA `:focus-visible` border reinforcement

5) QA artifact
- Added `docs/qa/perf-a11y-report.md` documenting:
  - scope and implementation details
  - command results
  - preview/Lighthouse blockers
  - manual follow-up checklist for keyboard/screen-reader and Lighthouse checks

Memory governance updates
- `docs/memory/decision-log.md`: appended `[DEC-007] Core Template Media Priority and Motion/Focus Hardening Policy`.
- `docs/memory/contracts.md`: appended `[CON-010] Core Template Performance and Accessibility Hardening Contract`.
- `docs/memory/risk-register.md`: appended `[RISK-007] Sandbox Limits Prevent Full Runtime A11y/Perf Audits`.
- `docs/memory/evidence-index.md`: appended `[EVD-013] Performance and Accessibility Hardening Verification`.
- `docs/memory/changelog.md`: appended `[CHG-013] Performance and Accessibility Hardening Pass`.
- Each appended entry includes NodeID, Date, Author, Change, and Status.

Required lint/typecheck/build verification (run after edits)
- `npm run check` -> PASS (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
- `npm run typecheck` -> PASS (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints).
- `npm run build` -> PASS; static routes generated successfully.

Node-required preview/Lighthouse verification status
- `npm run preview -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4322` -> blocked by sandbox: `listen EPERM: operation not permitted 127.0.0.1:4322`.
- Lighthouse attempt (`npx --yes lighthouse --version`) -> blocked by DNS/network: `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND registry.npmjs.org`.
- Fallback evidence captured via built artifacts and selector checks in `dist/**` and documented in `docs/qa/perf-a11y-report.md`.

Manual verification guidance
1. In an unrestricted environment, run `npm run build && npm run preview`.
2. Validate keyboard and screen-reader smoke checks on:
   - `/`
   - `/projects/atelier-scheduler/`
   - `/blog/astro-content-layer-in-practice/`
3. Run Lighthouse (mobile + desktop) for those routes and attach resulting scores.

Commit requirement status
- Attempted required single node commit:
  - `git add ... && git commit -m "node(n12_performance_and_accessibility_pass): performance and accessibility hardening pass"`
- Blocked by workspace state:
  - `fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git`
- Per governance, node was not failed solely for this environment-level commit blocker.

n13c_qa_run_and_blocker_fixes.title: Execute QA gate and fix release-blocking defects
n13c_qa_run_and_blocker_fixes.instruction: Goal: produce a release-ready result by executing the new automated checks and manual regression checklist, then remediating blockers. In-scope: run defined commands, perform checklist verification on primary routes, implement focused fixes for release-blocking defects found, and record evidence/results. Out-of-scope: non-blocking cosmetic improvements and broad refactors. Verification: quality-gate commands pass, checklist is completed with outcomes, and blocker fixes are validated. Expected artifacts: code/docs fixes as needed plus QA evidence updates in docs/memory files per governance.
n13c_qa_run_and_blocker_fixes.context: Must follow shared memory governance updates and include lint/typecheck/build evidence in completion report.
n13c_qa_run_and_blocker_fixes.report:
Completed `n13c_qa_run_and_blocker_fixes` by executing the scripted quality gate, running the manual release checklist against primary routes via static artifact evidence, remediating a release-blocking defect, and recording full evidence/memory updates.

What I changed
1) Executed QA gate and checklist run
- Ran `npm run quality:gate` successfully (check + typecheck + build + quality:test all passed).
- Attempted runtime preview verification via `npm run preview -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4322`, which failed due sandbox restriction: `listen EPERM`.
- Performed route-by-route checklist validation from generated `dist/**` artifacts and CSS contracts, then documented outcomes in:
  - `docs/qa/release-checklist-execution-2026-03-01.md`

2) Found and fixed a release-blocking defect
- Defect: published route HTML referenced `/images/...` assets that did not exist in repo/build output (`public/images` and `dist/images` absent), causing broken media on core routes.
- Fix implemented:
  - Added concrete placeholder assets under `public/images/blog/*.svg` and `public/images/projects/*.svg`.
  - Updated all published content `coverImage` paths to existing `.svg` assets:
    - `src/data/blog/{astro-content-layer-in-practice,tuning-page-motion-with-respect,from-scrapbook-motifs-to-portfolio-language}.md`
    - `src/data/projects/{atelier-scheduler,field-journal-cms,letterpress-notes,signalboard-analytics}.md`
  - Updated MDX inline figure path:
    - `src/data/blog/mdx-authoring-with-shared-prose-components.mdx`

3) Added validation evidence for blocker remediation
- Rebuilt output and ran an internal reference integrity sweep across all `dist/**/*.html` `href/src` links.
- Result: `All internal href/src references resolve within dist.`
